title: SIMD Vectorization — Lanes of Parallel Fire
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Explicit SIMD via std::simd or packed_simd can close the gap between Rust and Fortran for numerical hot paths
content.proof: Rust's autovectorizer is less mature than GCC/gfortran's. Explicit SIMD types (f64x4 for AVX2, f64x8 for AVX-512) give the compiler explicit lane packing. Combined with const generics for lane count, the same code adapts to target hardware at compile time.
content.benchmark: Autovectorized Rust: ~1.8B ops/sec. Explicit SIMD (projected): ~2.1B ops/sec. Fortran baseline: 2.27B.
content.caveat: Portable SIMD is still nightly-only. Production use requires either nightly or core::arch intrinsics per platform.
